title: Exelixis — Overview
html:
Exelixis Account Overview

Governing Thought

Exelixis is a pure-play oncology biotech with a singular strategic imperative: transition from the mature CABOMETYX (cabozantinib) franchise to zanzalintinib, its next-generation TKI currently in multiple Phase 3 trials. ZoomRx's relationship dates to 2019 (Cabozantinib PET) and has recently reactivated with campaign impact and message testing work around zanzalintinib — meaning ZoomRx is positioned at the exact moment Exelixis needs the most market research support: pre-launch and launch. Every BD opportunity on this account maps to the cabo-to-zanzalintinib pipeline transition, and the presence of active RFPs (ATU) and proposals (Branded Campaign Testing) signals that Exelixis is actively expanding its research vendor footprint right now. The window to deepen this relationship is open and time-bound — launch timing will dictate when budgets are committed.

The Exelixis Landscape: What You Need to Know

At a Glance

Dimension Detail
Company Exelixis, Inc. (EXEL, Nasdaq)
HQ Alameda, California
CEO Michael Morrissey, PhD
Focus Oncology — TKI franchise (cabozantinib, zanzalintinib)
Key commercial brand CABOMETYX (cabozantinib) — RCC, HCC, DTC
CABOMETYX FY 2025 net revenue ~$2.4B [UNVERIFIED — from user-provided context; not independently confirmed]
Lead pipeline asset Zanzalintinib (next-gen TKI) — Phase 3
ZoomRx relationship start ~2019 (Cabozantinib PET)
Active ZoomRx work Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact (2 waves); Proposals for ATU and Branded Campaign Testing
Account role Overseen account

Key dynamics:

  • Exelixis is a single-franchise company — CABOMETYX generates the vast majority of revenue, and zanzalintinib is the explicit successor
  • The company is in a critical pipeline-transition phase: every research dollar is increasingly pointed at zanzalintinib pre-launch and launch preparation
  • ZoomRx has historical credibility (Cabozantinib PET from 2019) and is being pulled back in for zanzalintinib-era work — a pattern that signals institutional memory and trust
  • Active RFPs and proposals indicate Exelixis is expanding its MR vendor footprint now

Company Context

Exelixis is an oncology-focused biopharmaceutical company built on the cabozantinib franchise. CABOMETYX (cabozantinib) is a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting MET, VEGFR, AXL, and RET — approved across three indications:

  • Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC): First-line (in combination with nivolumab or atezolizumab) and second-line monotherapy. RCC is the largest revenue driver.
  • Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC): Second-line monotherapy after sorafenib.
  • Differentiated Thyroid Cancer (DTC): Treatment of radioactive iodine-refractory DTC.

CABOMETYX also has a legacy formulation, COMETRIQ, for progressive metastatic medullary thyroid cancer. The cabozantinib franchise has been Exelixis's commercial engine since launch, and the company has built deep oncology field force capabilities around it.

Strategic reality: CABOMETYX faces patent expiry risk and increasing competition from next-generation IO/TKI combinations. Zanzalintinib is Exelixis's answer — a next-generation cabozantinib-derived TKI designed to improve on cabozantinib's efficacy and tolerability profile.

Portfolio: CABOMETYX + Zanzalintinib

CABOMETYX (cabozantinib) — The Commercial Engine

Dimension Detail
Mechanism Multi-kinase inhibitor (MET, VEGFR, AXL, RET)
Key indications RCC (1L combo, 2L mono), HCC (2L), DTC
FY 2025 revenue ~$2.4B [UNVERIFIED]
Competitive position Market leader in RCC TKI space; faces increasing IO combination competition
Key competitors Lenvima (lenvatinib, Eisai/Merck), Inlyta (axitinib, Pfizer), Sutent (sunitinib, Pfizer — declining), IO agents (nivolumab, pembrolizumab, avelumab)
ZoomRx history PET (2019), Integrated Tracking (2023)

Zanzalintinib — The Pipeline Successor

Dimension Detail
Mechanism Next-generation TKI — designed to improve on cabozantinib's profile
Stage Multiple Phase 3 trials
Key trials STELLAR-301 (HCC), STELLAR-302 (RCC) [UNVERIFIED trial names — from user-provided context]
Strategic importance This is the company's future. Exelixis has explicitly positioned zanzalintinib as the cabozantinib successor.
ZoomRx work Unbranded Campaign Impact (Wave 01 + 02, Feb 2026); ATU RFP (Apr 2026); Branded Campaign Testing proposal (Apr 2026)

Pipeline to Watch

Asset Mechanism Stage Why It Matters for ZoomRx
Zanzalintinib Next-gen TKI Phase 3 (STELLAR-301, STELLAR-302) The central BD opportunity. Every ZoomRx service maps to supporting this launch.
Cabozantinib combinations Cabo + IO combinations Various trials Lifecycle management for CABOMETYX may generate incremental research needs
Other Exelixis pipeline Various early-stage assets Preclinical/Phase 1 Long-term; Exelixis has stated ambition to build beyond cabozantinib/zanzalintinib. Monitor for future TA expansion.

ZoomRx Service Footprint

Service CABOMETYX (cabo) Zanzalintinib
PET / SFEA Historical (2019) --
Integrated Tracking Historical (2023) --
Perxcept -- Historical (2024)
Unbranded Campaign Impact -- Active (Wave 01 + 02)
ATU -- RFP received (Apr 2026)
Branded Campaign Testing -- Proposal submitted (v0.1, v0.2)
PET (launch) -- --
MSL / MEE -- --
HCP-Patient -- --
Digital Tracking -- --
Launch KPI -- --
OCE -- --

Key takeaway: ZoomRx's footprint is shifting from cabozantinib (historical PET, tracking) to zanzalintinib (campaign impact, ATU, branded campaign testing). The current active/pipeline work is all zanzalintinib-focused — exactly aligned with where Exelixis is spending. The white space is large: PET, MSL/MEE, Launch KPI, HCP-Patient, and Digital Tracking are all untapped on zanzalintinib.

Competitive Landscape Summary

Brand-Level Competitive Dynamics

Brand / Indication Primary Competitor Competitive Dynamic Implication for ZoomRx Work
CABOMETYX (RCC 1L) Lenvima + pembrolizumab (Eisai/Merck) KEYNOTE-581/CLEAR established lenvatinib + pembro as a key 1L RCC option; CABOMETYX combos (cabo + nivo, cabo + atezo) compete on similar endpoints Promotional effectiveness tracking matters because oncologists are choosing between similar IO/TKI combos based on rep messaging and clinical framing
CABOMETYX (RCC 2L) Inlyta (axitinib, Pfizer), Lenvima Second-line RCC is increasingly crowded; treatment sequencing decisions are complex HCP decision-making research (ATU, HCPP) would illuminate sequencing rationale
CABOMETYX (HCC) Lenvatinib (Eisai), Sorafenib (Bayer — declining), IO combos (atezo + bev) HCC treatment landscape evolving rapidly with IO-based 1L regimens pushing CABOMETYX later in the treatment sequence Zanzalintinib STELLAR-301 is targeting HCC — competitive tracking needed
Zanzalintinib (RCC) CABOMETYX (own franchise cannibalization), Lenvima, emerging TKIs, IO/TKI combos Zanzalintinib must prove superiority over cabozantinib AND competitors; managed cannibalization is the key commercial challenge Pre-launch ATU, PET, and message testing are all directly relevant — Exelixis needs to understand how to position the upgrade narrative
Zanzalintinib (HCC) Lenvatinib (Eisai), Sorafenib (legacy), IO combos STELLAR-301 targets HCC specifically; if approved, zanzalintinib competes in the same space as CABOMETYX but with (presumably) improved data Campaign impact and ATU work already underway for this reason

Vendor Competition (What We Know)

Area Status ZoomRx Position
PET Unknown current vendor (ZoomRx ran cabo PET in 2019) Historical relationship; re-entry opportunity on zanzalintinib launch
ATU RFP issued to ZoomRx (Apr 2026) Actively competing — likely multi-vendor RFP
Campaign Testing Proposal submitted (branded campaign testing) Active proposal; competitive status unknown
Unbranded Campaign Impact ZoomRx is the vendor Established on this workstream

What a New Principal Needs to Know (Fast Orientation)

  1. This is a one-franchise company in transition. Everything revolves around cabozantinib today and zanzalintinib tomorrow. There is no portfolio diversification to sell across. Every pitch must connect to the cabo-to-zanzalintinib narrative.

  2. The relationship is historical but recently reactivated. ZoomRx ran the Cabozantinib PET in 2019 and did integrated tracking in 2023, but the most active period is right now (2026) — with zanzalintinib campaign work and new RFPs. Institutional memory exists on both sides. Leverage it.

  3. The launch window is the BD window. Zanzalintinib is in Phase 3. Pre-launch and launch are when pharma companies spend the most on market research. Exelixis is already issuing RFPs (ATU) and reviewing proposals (branded campaign testing) — this is the moment to expand the footprint.

  4. RCC and HCC are the key TAs. Renal cell carcinoma is the revenue anchor; hepatocellular carcinoma is the expansion indication. Both are competitive, IO/TKI-driven markets where promotional effectiveness and physician decision-making research are high-value.

  5. Oncology field force work is a natural fit. Exelixis has a dedicated oncology field force built around CABOMETYX. MSL/MEE, FRM, and OCE tracking are all potential services — but none are currently active. This is a significant white-space opportunity.

  6. Win the ATU and branded campaign testing proposals. These are live, in-market opportunities. Winning them establishes ZoomRx as the zanzalintinib research partner during the most commercially important phase.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
